why: The incubator has domain-specific evaluation logic around venture selection, validation speed, leverage, and resilience.
The incubator benefits from domain-specific suggestions because the next best move depends on current bottleneck type.
The incubator needs packets and watchtower pages that separate exploratory ideas from operator-credible doctrine.
Real-world usefulness can be defined through live validation, reusable assets, trust discipline, and portfolio learning.
risks_if_forced_into_chip: If the portfolio remains too broad, the chip can become startup theater scoring instead of a real operating system.
If human gates are removed, the chip can create reputational and trust damage faster than it creates leverage.

mission.goal: Run a narrow, high-output incubator for vibe-coded startups with one operator plus agentic systems.
mission.realworld_outcome: Launch and validate ventures faster while preserving trust, auditability, and reusable portfolio learning.
success_criteria: Ventures reach live customer validation faster than an ad hoc founder workflow.
The incubator reuses prompts, code, workflows, and doctrine across ventures.
Weekly reviews produce clear continue, narrow, pivot, or stop decisions.
Investor-facing and sensitive workflows have reviewable audit trails.
The operator can manage the active portfolio without hidden staffing assumptions.

chip_boundary.why_external_chip: The incubator needs domain-local evaluation and operating doctrine that should not leak into Spark core.

must_not_do: claim live market proof without live evidence
auto-promote heuristic results into real-world validated doctrine
open random new venture lanes without portfolio-cap awareness
present stale or invented portfolio state as truth

always_on_stages: ingest weekly updates and KPI snapshots
route build, research, GTM, or trust tasks
routing_notes: Do not add more active ventures when the portfolio has unresolved resilience or learning gaps.
Prioritize fixing the strongest venture's current bottleneck before opening unrelated new themes.
Investor readiness only activates after validation and trust thresholds are met.

source_registry: official accelerator mechanics from Y Combinator, Techstars, and Antler
packet_schema_notes: Every promoted lesson should state mechanism, boundary, evidence lane, and next recommended use.
Every failure review should capture the bottleneck, missed assumptions, and reusable correction.
contradiction_policy: Prefer live customer and operator outcomes over heuristic scores.
When two ventures disagree, keep both visible until the evidence lane breaks the tie.
Security or trust failures override growth-positive signals.
realworld_validation_surface: active usage from a real operator team
measurable time saved, revenue created, or reliability improved
clear proof the loop can run with one operator plus agentic support

autoloop_mastery_policy.mastery_definition: The incubator reliably turns venture ideas into validated, reusable, and trust-worthy operating assets with one operator plus agentic systems.
autoloop_mastery_policy.focus_pressure: High. Keep the portfolio narrow and force weekly bottleneck resolution.
autoloop_mastery_policy.expansion_pressure: Low until at least one venture is clearly validated and one more is repeatably near-best.
autoloop_mastery_policy.pressure_test_pressure: High on willingness-to-pay, trust risk, and founder throughput assumptions.
autoloop_mastery_policy.usefulness_pressure: Maximum. No doctrine promotion without evidence that changes a real venture outcome.
queue_policy: Cap active ventures at a number the operator can review honestly.
Prefer deepening the best venture over scattering effort across new themes.
Archive stalled ventures explicitly instead of leaving them in the active queue.

anti_drift_risks: mistaking prototype velocity for company creation
portability_notes: General venture lessons should stay chip-local until they transfer across multiple ventures.
Trust and capital workflows should remain human-supervised even if tooling becomes shared.
rollback_or_containment: freeze new admissions if resilience gaps accumulate
drop a venture to advisory-only if trust review fails
revert doctrine promotion if later live evidence contradicts it

required_outputs: valid chip manifest and project config
stable supporting metrics and bottleneck labels
memory documents for evidence, doctrine, boundary, and frontier
clear difference between exploratory and stronger evidence lanes
failure_conditions: The chip implies live proof from heuristic-only runs.
Watchtower pages disagree with ledger state.
The system opens more ventures than the operator can govern.
Investor workflows activate before customer validation and trust review.

next_build_steps: Build the program control plane with venture registry, weekly update ingestion, and decision packets.
Add venture execution primitives: experiments, artifacts, KPI snapshots, and bottleneck routing.
Add customer and GTM workflows before deep investor automation.
Add trust review and data-room scaffolding before scaling external ventures.
Promote repeated cross-venture lessons into doctrine only after live evidence.
